title: Real Estate Cap Rates by City 2026 | firemaths.info
description: Average capitalization rates for rental properties across 50+ US cities. Essential data for real estate investors comparing markets.

@graph.headline: Real Estate Cap Rates by City 2026
@graph.description: Average capitalization rates for rental properties across 50+ US cities. Essential data for real estate investors comparing markets.

mainEntity.name: What is a good cap rate in 2026?

acceptedAnswer.text: A good cap rate typically ranges from 4% to 8% depending on the asset class and location. In lower-risk markets (like New York or San Francisco), cap rates sit around 2-4%. In higher-risk or emerging markets, you can find cap rates of 6-8%.

mainEntity.name: Which US city has the highest cap rate?

acceptedAnswer.text: Based on our 2026 analysis, Detroit, MI has the highest average cap rate among major metros at 5.20%, driven by lower median home prices relative to rents.

mainEntity.name: How do you calculate cap rate?

acceptedAnswer.text: Cap Rate = Net Operating Income (NOI) / Current Market Value of the property. NOI is calculated by taking gross annual rental income and subtracting all operating expenses (taxes, insurance, maintenance, property management, vacancy).

mainEntity.name: Are cap rates going up or down in 2026?

acceptedAnswer.text: Cap rates have stabilized in 2026 after expanding during the higher interest rate environments of previous years. Property values have adjusted, and rental demand remains strong in growing metros.

mainEntity.name: Does a higher cap rate mean a better investment?

acceptedAnswer.text: Not always. A higher cap rate usually indicates higher risk or lower expected property appreciation. Lower cap rates (like in premium coastal cities) often reflect perceived safety and strong historical appreciation.
